concept.title: What you're customizing (HTML5 transform architecture)
concept.shortdesc: DITA-OT publishing is a pipeline. Understanding its three phases — preprocessing, XSLT transformation, and post-processing — is essential before writing any override.

ul.li: copying/transient file preparation in the temp folder

conbody.p: Why this matters: Many "mysterious" HTML issues are not XSLT bugs; they come from preprocessing (e.g., conref not resolved, chunking not applied, keys not in scope).
After preprocessing, the HTML5 pipeline runs XSLT to generate:

ul.li: navigation artifacts such as TOC pages
HTML element mappings for DITA structures (p, section, note, steps, tables, xref, etc.)

conbody.p: Your XSLT customizations live here. This is where you:

ul.li: add CSS classes and data-* attributes
change output for specific DITA elements

conbody.p: After HTML is generated, DITA-OT typically:

ul.li: writes support files (images, JS if configured/added)

conbody.p: Why this matters: Your output may look wrong simply because your CSS/JS wasn't copied into output.

p.b: Extension points: the supported integration points

conbody.p: DITA-OT exposes official extension points that let you plug into the pipeline without editing core files.

ul.li: dita.xsl.html5 — override or extend the main HTML5 XSLT step
dita.xsl.html5.toc — override or extend TOC generation XSLT
dita.conductor.html5.param and dita.conductor.html5.toc.param — pass parameters into the main HTML5 and TOC steps
general plug-in XSLT import extension points (import-based override mechanism)

conbody.p: The key idea: create a plug-in that imports the default HTML5 XSLT and overrides only the templates you need.
